channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Donald Trump uses 'Full Metal Jacket' clips to portray his ideal military
item.description: Donald Trump is embracing perhaps Hollywood’s most memorable drill sergeant to portray his vision of a hardened military and mock the Biden administration’s embrace of the LGBTQ+ community serving openly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida government finds fault with abortion ballot measure over ads and petitions
item.description: Florida's government has threatened TV stations over an ad promoting an abortion-rights ballot measure and is now alleging fraud in petitions to get it before voters.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump opens up largest betting lead since days after Biden's dropout
item.description: Former President Trump has opened up a betting lead over Vice President Kamala Harris in the race for president of over 10 points for the first time since July 31.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trio of new polls agree on where Trump and Harris stand with 3 weeks to go until Election Day
item.description: With just over three weeks to go until Election Day, a trio of new national polls in the White House race indicate former President Trump erasing recent gains made by Vice President Kamala Harris.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Biden warned Iran that killing Trump would be an act of war: report
item.description: President Biden reportedly instructed his National Security Council to make clear to Iran that any attempt on former President Trump’s life would be viewed as an act of war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris campaign deploys Bill Clinton to key states 22 days from Election Day
item.description: Bill Clinton is campaigning for Kamala Harris in the key battlegrounds of Georgia and North Carolina just three weeks shy of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia election workers move closer to collecting Rudy Giuliani’s assets
item.description: Two Georgia election workers are pushing to collect their $146 million defamation judgment against Rudy Giuliani, but the former New York City mayor is digging in amid an array of financial troubles.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In outreach to Black men, Harris to vow to legalize weed, protect crypto
item.description: Polls show that some Black men may be gravitating toward former President Trump or not vote at all. Vice President Harris and other prominent Democrats are trying to counter that.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: This could have been a year of a federal court reckoning for Trump. Judges had other ideas
item.description: A year that began with the prospect of a federal court reckoning for Trump will conclude without any chance at trial, leaving voters without an up-or-down jury verdict in the two most consequential cases against the Republican presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What we know about arrest outside Trump’s California rally
item.description: Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, a noted supporter of Trump, discussed safety measures taken at the Saturday event.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic voter registration raises red flags for Harris
item.description: Democrats’ voter registration advantage has dropped in three key battleground states — Pennsylvania, North Carolina and Nevada — raising a red flag for Vice President Harris as experts cite a lack of enthusiasm for the Biden administration brand and the Democratic Party, generally, as problems.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What $35 trillion in US national debt means for Americans
item.description: With the national debt standing at more than $35 trillion, economists fear that the country's debt load will only grow
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump holds a rally in California, a state he's almost certain to lose
item.description: The White House election is expected to come down to battlegrounds such as Wisconsin and Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Rebuilding Florida after Hurricane Milton and protecting against scams
item.description:
Recovery efforts can unite communities after catastrophic damage. Neighbors come together to help one another, while state officials and law enforcement work diligently to restore order. Gradually, residents begin to see their homes return to normalcy.
But even with the good, comes the bad.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Walz returns to Mankato to reprise role as coach, deliver football pep talk and campaign a little
item.description: The governor stopped through on a campaign visit marking the 25th anniversary of the season the Mankato West football team won the state championship with him as assistant coach. He will attend the Governors Pheasant opener in Sleepy Eye Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Maria Torres-Springer discusses new role as NYC’s first deputy mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday appointed Maria Torres-Springer to be New York City’s new first deputy mayor after Sheena Wright resigned from her position.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Battle to shore up bases remains as Election Day nears
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guest talks about deadly conflicts in the Middle East and how those have factored into domestic politics. Plus, we follow up with two Generation Z voters about how they’re approaching the election.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Michigan newspaper issues correction after Trump claims he won Man of Year award
item.description: A Michigan newspaper issued a correction after former President Donald Trump cited the paper at a speech in Detroit claiming he was Michigan’s “Man of the Year” in 2023.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Senate candidates steer clear of debates
item.description: Senate candidates this cycle are largely steering clear of the debate stage, in some cases avoiding it altogether, marking a major change from past years as campaigns question the debates' importance. For years, it was common for top Senate races to feature multiple debates.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon could resign: sources
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Less than a month after the NYPD's interim commissioner was appointed, another major shake-up could be coming. Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon is expected to step down, sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Emily Stevens discusses accountability, safety, and fiscal responsibility on the Clark County School District Board
item.description: Emily Stevens, candidate for the Clark County School District Board of Trustees, appeared on 8 News Now's Daily Voting Guide to discuss why she believes she's the right fit for the open seat in District A.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ryan Hamilton discusses homelessness, the Animal Foundation, and F1
item.description: Ryan Hamilton, a Republican candidate for the Clark County Commission, appeared on 8 News Now's Daily Voting Guide to share his perspective on homelessness, animal welfare, and Formula One's impact on the local economy as the election nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mayor Frey vetoes carbon emission fee adopted by city council
item.description: In his veto letter Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey said he agrees with the aims of the fee on polluters, but he said the council’s action amounts to an “unauthorized tax.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: The presidential campaigns have made over 200 visits but speak to just a fraction of American voters
item.description: The nation's unique Electoral College system for electing a president, which replaces the popular vote, puts disproportionate voting power in the hands of a relative few states.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
